reviews.review_text:
This game is a wonderful time capsule from 2008. That includes the political and economic climate at the time, the "brown grey and realistic" aesthetic, as well as the PC port quality (which is abysmal and requires third party fixes to run at modern standards). Rockstar still had something to prove when they were making this one. 

Whether it's better than GTA V or not is debatable, and people do, but I personally think it's the best game in the series. With fusionfix and the radio downgrader that restores cut songs from the soundtrack, this game provides an experience that no other game can. This was Rockstar's first ever attempt at making a game that nobody else was able to at a time when everybody and their mother were still trying to make a clone of the older GTA classics that had already taken the world by storm. Almost 20 years later, I don't think any other game dev has managed to even get close to matching its quality. Many have tried, but nobody paid attention to the little things more than 2008 Rockstar did.

It loses a lot of features from San Andreas, which many people (myself included) considered a major downgrade at the time, but it offers an unforgettable story, amazing fictional version of NYC, ridiculous but still entirely believable characters, and the best physics for combat and driving that still haven't been matched to this day except for RDR1. I put many, many more hours into GTA V and their other games, but IV goes for quality over quantity every step of the way.

Tons of people complain about the driving physics, but they're the reason I constantly return to this game. The sense of speed and the control you need to have border on racing sim territory compared to any other game of its type, and I spend most of my time just racing around at max speed and crashing into stuff. Older muscle cars are made of steel and don't damage easily (but you're flying out the windshield), the new pop can aluminum ones crumple, old cars lack ABS, 4WD, FWD and RWD cars all handle entirely differently, and there's no artificial cap on the max speed the cars can go like in GTA V. The attention to every detail of the driving was a game changer. Never gets old. 

The Ballad of Gay Tony is also arguably one of the best game expansions ever made.

10/10, would reinstall and play every year again.
There's never been a better steal car and drive over people simulator than this one, Check out FusionFix to make stuff better 10/10

Hi... I am writing this review after playing for over 100 hours, and I can confidently say that GTA IV has the best story in the entire GTA franchise. I loved the game so much that I grinded out 100% of the Steam achievements.

Everyone should play this masterpiece at least once to see what Rockstar cooked up before GTA V. However, if you are playing on a modern PC, there are a few things you need to know:

1. DOWNLOAD FUSIONFIX:
Before you even start the game, download the FusionFix mod pack. The original PC port is notoriously broken on modern hardware. If you play with an uncapped framerate (anything above 60 FPS), the game engine physically breaks. Quick-time events fail, minigames (like the dancing in the DLC) become impossible to beat, and the final mission can permanently soft-lock. FusionFix patches these engine bugs, fixes the lighting, and gives you a smooth, glitch-free experience.

2. THE DLCs ARE MANDATORY:
Just completing the base game with Niko is not enough. You absolutely need to play the two included DLC episodes (The Lost and Damned and The Ballad of Gay Tony). They aren't just extra missions; they contain massive, overlapping stories that directly connect to the main game. Playing all three campaigns is the only way to experience the complete story of Liberty City.

3. THE DRIVING PHYSICS:
Be prepared for the driving to feel very different if you are coming from GTA V. The cars here are heavy and have realistic suspension. It takes an hour or two to get used to, but once you master it, the car chases are some of the most satisfying in the series.

100% highly recommended. If you love open-world games with gritty, deep narratives, this is an absolute must-play.

My earliest experience of the Grand Theft Auto series was watching my Uncle Scott run around blasting people in GTA 3. The game was so far beyond anything I had ever imagined as a little kidlet as well as being ahead of the curve visually. It ran perfectly as intended on the PS2. Gone are those days where products actually work.

I do not recommend this product to anyone and I am thoroughly shocked that more people are just glancing over the fact that it's a faulty product. The reason being as the product itself does not work and requires the user to mod the product in order to play it and this should never be the case from a AAA game company. Rockstar also integrated there launcher and if you have had accounts on other platforms there is no way to seamlessly link those. Being in the launcher counts towards play time as well. So take that into account if you intend on testing the product and are considering a refund.

Thankfully, there are many resources out there for you to quickly get your game actually running. Fusionfix and Gillian's GTA IV Modpack will get your game actually running past the opening menu, without stuttering, as mostly intended and are easy to source and mod to your game (literally drag and drop into your directory) with guides on the inteded settings and potential bugs and fixes you might encounter on their respective websites. The longest part about this process was the throttled download but really only takes about 5-10 minutes to mod and test everything.

Without these mods you barely make it past the first loading screen, if you do you are met with constant stuttering, performance issues, crashes and disappointment. 

Thank you to the amazing mod-community for fixing this game!

https://gillian-guide.github.io/drag-and-drop-archive/

https://github.com/ThirteenAG/GTAIV.EFLC.FusionFix?tab=readme-ov-file#readme

this game used to be good now it randomly kicks you out during missions and cutscenes and randomly when your playing for the rockstar launcher to ask you to sign in again which is weird because in the illegally downloaded versions that never happens and its so much more stable but if you pay for it then u have to deal with it, ill never pay for another rockstar game

Grand Theft Auto IV: The Complete Edition was a huge part of my childhood, especially during the PlayStation 3 era, where I spent countless hours both in the story mode and online. Looking back at it today, I still consider it one of the strongest Grand Theft Auto games Rockstar has ever made, not because it was perfect, but because of the identity and atmosphere it managed to create.

Compared to previous GTA titles, especially Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas, GTA IV represented a massive technological evolution. The jump in graphics, physics, animations, and overall immersion was impressive at the time. Liberty City felt alive in a much more grounded and realistic way, and that realism became one of the defining aspects of the experience.

What makes GTA IV stand out the most for me is its story and the construction of Niko Bellic as a character. Unlike Grand Theft Auto V, which feels more exaggerated and cinematic in its approach, GTA IV carries a darker and heavier tone throughout the entire experience. Niko isn’t just another criminal protagonist, he’s someone haunted by his past, by war, betrayal, revenge, and the consequences of the things he has done. There’s an emotional weight to the story that GTA V never managed to replicate in the same way.

The atmosphere of Liberty City also plays a huge role in this. Even though GTA V has a much larger map, GTA IV feels denser and more focused. There’s a certain feeling to Liberty City that makes exploration memorable, whether it’s the darker visual tone, the interactions between characters, or simply the grounded way the world is presented.

Another aspect that often gets overlooked is how much detail and interaction GTA IV actually had. Features like internet cafés, emails, browsing websites, and the integration of those systems into the world made the game feel immersive in ways that later titles simplified or abandoned. GTA IV may have had less side content compared to San Andreas, but what was there felt more connected to the identity of the game.

At the same time, this is also where some of its limitations become noticeable. Compared to San Andreas, GTA IV reduced a lot of the sandbox freedom and customization. Vehicle customization was almost nonexistent, there were fewer activities, and overall the game felt more restricted in terms of player freedom. San Andreas offered a wider variety of systems and interactions, and that’s something GTA IV never fully replaced despite its technological improvements.

The driving physics are another divisive aspect. Many players disliked the heavier and more realistic handling of vehicles, but personally I think it added personality to the game and matched the grounded tone Rockstar was aiming for.

The expansions, especially Grand Theft Auto IV: The Ballad of Gay Tony and Grand Theft Auto IV: The Lost and Damned, helped expand Liberty City even further and remain some of the best DLCs Rockstar has ever produced. They gave more perspective to the city and its characters, making the overall experience feel more complete.

Looking back, GTA IV feels like a turning point for the franchise. GTA V evolved the formula in some ways, especially with online content and long-term support, but it also lost part of the grounded identity and technical ambition that made GTA IV so memorable when it originally released.

In the end, GTA IV isn’t the most content-rich GTA game, and it doesn’t offer the same freedom as San Andreas, but it delivers one of the strongest atmospheres and narratives in the entire franchise. It’s darker, more personal, and more emotionally grounded than the games that followed, and that’s exactly why it still stands out today.

Score: 7/10

i love this game and i bought it recently on sale so i could ditch my pirated copy, but damn the performance sucks on this. the optimization is honestly hot garbage even with mods installed.

i have a low-end laptop, my graphics are all set to low at 1366x720, and even with this setup my pirated copy runs better close to 30fps (+ offline mode). but on the legitimate version... i only get around 15-22fps with the same settings, and my cpu and system memory usage jumps up by A LOT. i believe the culprit must be having to have both steam and rgl running in the background, because they already use up so much memory, which could've meant more for the actual game to use.

hell, my total hours on this game on steam were mostly spent by having to test and benchmark the game with the optimization mods i installed. in the end, it looks like i'll go back to playing on my pirated copy. when i upgrade my setup, i'll be sure to give gta 4 a try again and update my review.

This will remain my favorite GTA game of all time. Being unique in the fact it has 2 full separate story DLCs, being The Lost and Damned and The Ballad of Gay Tony, complete with their own collectables, unique vehicles, gameplay mechanics, activities, and weapons will keep you playing for quite a while. The game's unique physics and ragdoll engines make GTA IV and it's DLCs a truly unique game in the GTA franchise.

The game at base runs alright on PC, though cutscenes are a bit zoomed in. Some light modding will fix this however.

The optimization was awful. In GTA 5 I had more frames, even though it was a newer game with a bigger map and better graphics. I also struggled with the last mission because I couldn't escape the helicopter as Niko due to a bug.

  First thing Fusionfix Mod and Radio restoration mod is a must. Niko Bellic and Liberty City is the LITERAL BEST GTA has to offer. This is by far the best game in the franchise. i was 13 when this dropped in 08 and this game still has me blown away in story and world building. That theme, hits you like no other, the physics is unmatched as of May 13th, 2026. The Music in this game doesnt get enough credit, the radio hits everytime. How vice city was a period piece of the mid 80s Gta4 is a period piece and a very damn good one of what life was like in 2008 NYC. Niko is well written, nearly the best outside of R*'s Arthur Morgan in Red Ded Redmption2. His story is compelling in the best unique way of experiencing the 'American Dream' fresh off the boat and rock bottom in a major American City. Spider-man NYC wish it was a tenth of what Rockstar created with Liberty City, truly a more authentic take. Episodes of Liberty City DLC are also pretty good too, but this review is mainly about GTA IV on its own merit. This is by all definition a true Master Class of Digital art and amazing feat made in gaming back in 2008, and as i write this still stands as one of the GREATEST GAMES OF ALL TIME. Must buy Gamers Game 100%

GTA IV + Episodes from Liberty City is Rockstar at its darkest, grittiest, and most cinematic. As a huge GTA fan, I still think Liberty City feels insanely alive — dirty streets, heavy physics, iconic radio, unforgettable characters, and that perfect crime-drama atmosphere.

Niko’s story is deep and emotional, Johnny’s biker chapter is raw and tragic, and Luis brings the flashy chaos with clubs, money, and explosions. Together, they show three different sides of the same legendary city.

It may be darker and more serious than other GTA games, but that’s exactly what makes it special. GTA IV + Episodes is a masterpiece and one of the best GTA experiences ever.

my least favorite Rockstar game by far.

the story starts out having so much potential only for it to quickly devolve into a gun for hire simulator. very few characters are actually memorable because you dont even get to know them, every mission is just them sending Niko to kill someone or steal something for money, especially in the second half of the game. in fact, most of these missions could be assigned to literally any other character and there wouldnt be a difference because they're all practically the same.

the gameplay is definitely subjective but i did not enjoy it at all, the cars feel like boats and require some getting used to, bikes are just not fun to ride even after learning how to ride them, and shooting is the most unsatisfying thing ever. a very annoying thing is that there is auto saving in this game but at the very start of the mission instead of places that make sense, so in a mission where you drive for 10 minutes and there is a shooting section or a police chase in the end and you die there, you have to start again from the very start.

Lastly, this might be just the Steam version i dont know, but this game runs terribly, despite my computer being able to run relatively modern games with no issues, including GTA 5 Enhanced, it struggles to run this game at anything above 25fps at 1920/1080 with constant frame drops.

The only thing impressive about Rockstar these days is how they manage to make even their classic library of revolutionary titles not worth paying for.

This is a complete waste of money. I paid full price for this just to find out that not only did they gut the online features that could've easily worked through P2P and the private/solo sessions that were always available, but they've somehow made zero optimizations to this twenty year old game that might justify such an oddly gimped re-release. It still has odd performance issues on PC, cutscenes jitter, and the game starts in 2008 potato mode. Your first ten to twenty minutes of gameplay are staring at the options menu trying to get the game to work properly on your PC.

And then; on top of all of that: You have to be able to connect to Rockstar's services to even play the thing. You know, the single player game with all its online functionality completely removed. Great deal for someone like me who's internet is currently unreliable, definitely don't regret spending money on and downloading something I won't be able to play in a few days.

Yeah I put in a refund request. You're better off "acquiring" via other means. At least then you'll be able to reliably run it. This isn't even worth the shady key site treatment thanks to the Rockstar DRM.

Achievements were also added too!

My only complaints are:

[olist]
    [*]Online got removed when the patch that replaced [b] Games for Windows LIVE [/b] with [b] Rockstar Games Launcher [/b] was released in 2020.

    [*][i] The Ballad of Gay Tony [/i] has a worst story that the main game and [i] The Lost and Damned [/i] combined. Also all missions are designed to be so annoying and unenjoyable.

    [*]Main game has a superb story, but most mission's structure is recycled. At least, it got fixed on DLCs.

This is my most replayed [i] Grand Theft Auto [/i] ! First on a cousin's [b] PlayStation 3 [/b] , later on a friend's [b] PlayStation 3 [/b], then on another cousin's [b] XBOX 360 [/b] , and finally on my [b] PC [/b] a couple of times.
[h3] I'll never be tired of this game! [/h3]

[b]It's a game worth your time! I love the story - it's really deep, emotional and even funny sometimes.
The dating system is great in this game, maybe not perfect but for a GTA game it's cool imo. Also the Strip Club is cut down in features e.g. touching the stripper... Not that I care about it that much but it would be nice to have that feature because it's present in GTA V for example (I heard that it was in GTA: San Andreas too, but at time of writing this review, I haven't played that legendary classic yet so I can't really tell if that's true). The only problem with this game is its performance on PC - it's really terrible, even (or should I say especially) for modern systems. You can install FusionFix like me though and everything should be working fine, however be aware of potential bugs & glitches caused by high framerate (e.g. in cutscenes - for me it once didn't work properly but overall it's good now).[/b]
[i]I personally prefer this game over GTA V / GTA Online but that's my preference.[/i]

GTA 4 is a great story wrapped in a terrible game.

One of the most frustrating games I've ever played.  I have a love-hate relationship with it.

I know it's an 18 year old game lol.  I know most won't read, let alone agree with this.  Still, I just wrapped up the base game and all the DLC and I'm ready to rip out an unpopular opinion.

Niko is a great character.  All the side characters are at least interesting, or so annoying or evil that you love to hate them.  The dialogue and voice acting is high-effort and really enjoyable.  Virtually everything else surrounding those things is absolute garbage.

Let's get the PC port out of the way; it's garbage.  Game runs poorly and microstutters/hitches even when running at a high FPS on a high end PC.  When the FPS is locked, it still runs terribly.  I used the latest version of the FusionFix mod, and the performance was still lousy.  It would also hard lock and crash every so often.  Three times, my screen froze and prevented an alt-trab-delete, forcing me to hard restart my PC.  I found out this was tied to an FPS/Draw distance bug.  Absolute trash.  

Moving on foot is so clunky and over-animated, it almost made me quit the game multiple times.  In the middle of combat, the protag will just wander aimlessly, move at a light jog, stick like glue to cover, highjack the wrong vehicle when standing directly near what you want him to take, etc.  Every single action you take is so overly-animated that it can take a full 10 seconds just to stop and get out of a car.  When the car is on fire, this is a huge deal.  When this is the fourth time you're doing a mission because the clunkiness already got you killed twice and your pc crashed once, and there's no checkpoint system meaning you have to completely start the mission over with low health, no armor and low ammo, you'll see how outright horrendous the gameplay is.

I could rant more about the game, but I can sum it up as follows:  the devs made absolutely no concessions for gameplay or QOL.  Every time there was a conflict between realism/immersion and QOL, the devs chose realism.  The friendship system (friends calling you every 1-2 minutes and punishing you for not going bowling with them) is madness.  The devs purposely frustrate the player because they think it adds immersion, putting you in Niko's shoes.  Madness I say.  The thing has to work as a game first, then you can worry about fully animating a horses balls.  Watching your character roll around on the ground and break dance while on fire for 10 full seconds, in the middle of a gunfight while you're getting shot, on your 4th try of a terribly scripted on the rails mission with no checkpoint, will make you want to end it all.

Looks like I went on a rant anyway.  Who cares, this is therapeutic for me.  The only saving grace for this game is how well written it is.  Niko is an interesting guy.  His convos with people are great.  Rockstar can write some very funny scenes when they aren't forcing you to fly a terribly controlled helicopter through a skyscraper for the 8th time.  I HATE HELICOPTERS.

I played RDR1 and 2, also GTA 5.  None of those games pissed me off like this.  It's wildly overrated because of the Reddit circle-j*rk hivemind that determines what is and isn't allowed to be criticized.  Screw this game lmao.
Poorly optimised and crappy, It's literally unplayable in some parts of the map even if u have high specs.
O jogo não tem o que falar, ele é excelente, só poderia ser uma jogo melhor otimizado para os computadores

This is the best and most underrated gta games, although i have 79 hours i can tell you, the physics and overall guns and graphics? AMAZING! WOULD RECOMMEND!
dont really like the vehicle driving. Love everything else. Maybe the graphics could be improved but its an old game so its definitely understandable.

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]
Unlike other GTA games, the people we work with become our friends, and to maintain good relationships with them, we sometimes go out to eat, sometimes hang out, and even take side missions from them to earn money. We can also go on dates with the women we meet. We can even meet these women on the internet. Unfortunately, there is no option to buy cars in the game; those advertisements on the internet are just for show. Sometimes we run into random characters on the street and can do side quests for them. The map size is quite good, but I recommend lowering the traffic density in the game settings because there are too many cars on the roads. As you complete missions, new safehouses and shops open up. You can go there to buy new clothes and weapons. 
[h2] Graphics [/h2]
The game is in a better state with the definitive graphics, but the FPS drops abnormally in certain places while playing. I also noticed a significant amount of graphical glitches, especially while driving. The graphics look bad in cinematics, but that is quite normal for a game like this old.
[h2] Audio [/h2]
The radio stations are decent and have a good variety of music. However, the voice acting for the characters is truly great and worth praising. Niko's voice was different than I expected at first, but I adapted to it quickly. Gun sounds and city noises are fine enough to do the job. 
[h2] Story [/h2]
We play as "Niko Bellic," a Serbian war veteran who arrives illegally in America by ship. Our cousin "Roman" always talked about his American adventures, which made us want to come here. Of course, things are not as they were told; our cousin lied to us the whole time. Throughout the story, we get involved with the dirty and illegal groups of Liberty City, fulfilling their orders in exchange for money, fame, and information. What we are truly looking for is to find a few suspicious names who betrayed us and our friends during the war, cooperated with the enemy, and set an ambush, so we can get our revenge. As things deepen, we gain a respectable position, even becoming a hitman for official institutions working on behalf of the state. Liberty City is a place where different mafia groups, street gangsters, corrupt politicians, and the bureaucracy tear each other apart, and we take advantage of this by handling their dirty work. 
[h2] Difficulty [/h2]
The game is generally easy. The only thing that will challenge you is escaping from the police at 5 and 6-star wanted levels. There were no complex things like puzzles in the story missions, and GTA is not that kind of game anyway. Therefore, there is nothing I can call difficult.

Not sure what to say that's already been covered, but Rockstar definitely went full in with Grand Theft Auto IV. The dark gritty story, physics, the characters/cultures you meet and see along the way, the typical unfiltered humor and the soundrack that shaped some of my musical taste just to mention a few things. Of course, Niko Bellic is a well written protagonist too. The game felt more real in a way, it's a balance between old and new and captures a certain time of the 2000's. GTA 4 is the HBO Original of video games, I'm sure if you have seen series like The Sopranos and The Wire etc you know exactly what I'm talking about (which they did take some insperation from). The DLC's were alright as well even if they might not have had the same impact. Wish sometimes I could experience this masterpiece all over again like it's the first time. All GTA games are great in their own way and there's probably one that reconates with you in a more specific way, and that for me would be IV.

First 3 Hours in GTA IV – Already Hooked

I've only played around 3 hours of Grand Theft Auto IV so far, but I'm already having a great time.

The story grabbed my attention quickly, and Niko Bellic feels like a genuinely interesting protagonist. The atmosphere of Liberty City is amazing—it's gritty, detailed, and feels alive in a way that still impresses me today.

The driving takes some getting used to, but once it clicks, it's actually really fun. The physics and overall realism make the game feel different from other GTA titles I've played.

Even though I've barely scratched the surface, I can already see why so many people consider this one of the best games in the series.

If you've been thinking about trying GTA IV, I'd definitely recommend giving it a chance. So far, it's been well worth it.

Playtime: 3 hours
Recommendation: 👍 Yes

First off, the Fusion Fix mod is REQUIRED to play GTA4. As it fixes a lot of issues with the base game and provides of some additional display options. Aside from that, it is a classic GTA game worth playing. I find it better than GTA5 and gives off this nestogic Y2K era.

GTA IV has one of the best stories Rockstar has ever made.

Liberty City feels dark, realistic, and alive in a way that still stands out today. The atmosphere, physics, soundtrack, and attention to detail make the world feel incredibly immersive.

Niko Bellic is also one of Rockstar’s greatest protagonists. His story feels more personal and emotional compared to other GTA games, and that’s what makes GTA IV special. It’s not just chaos and explosions — it actually has depth.

The driving may feel heavier than GTA V, but honestly it fits the realistic tone perfectly. And the ragdoll physics are still hilarious even years later.

A darker, more grounded GTA experience that aged far better than people give it credit for.

**Recommended**
Grand Theft Auto IV is easily one of the most immersive open-world games I've ever played. While newer GTA titles focus on bigger maps and more activities, GTA IV stands out because of its grounded story, realistic atmosphere, and memorable characters. After CJ Niko Bellic is one of best GTA protagonists. His journey from arriving in Liberty City with dreams of a better life to getting pulled deeper into crime feels authentic and emotional. The writing is surprisingly mature & balancing dark theme. The driving physics may feel different at first if you are coming from GTA 5. But once you get used to them, they make every chase and crash feel realistic. Liberty City itself is fantastic, packed with detail, personality, and a gritty urban vibe that still holds up today. Some missions can feel repetitive, and the constant phone calls from friends can get annoying. Even after all these years, GTA IV remains a masterpiece of storytelling and world-building. If you're looking for a GTA game with a darker tone, realistic atmosphere, and an unforgettable protagonist, this is absolutely worth playing.

I first saw this game in an internet cafe when I was around 6 or 7 years old. An older guy was playing it, and the game literally mesmerized me with its visuals and engine. It made me say, "Can a game really be like this?" I asked him to launch this game for me too and played it for a short while. A few years later, after we bought the game for our own PC at home, I had the opportunity to play it. At that age, I didn't understand anything about the game's story. The only things I cared about were fighting with people, rolling them down stairs, pushing them off cliffs, driving cars, and making people hit me so I could watch them get arrested by the police. These things gave me incredible pleasure. When I replayed the game after growing up, I saw how realistic, dark, and close to our world its story is among the GTA games. I think it has the best story in the series. Even in this era, the game engine still feels very good. A production that everyone should play.
